echo "=== Verification Report for DiscoveryServer -> XDSServer Rename ==="

echo ""

    55→	// debounceMax is the maximum time to wait for events
    56→	// while debouncing. Defaults to 10 seconds. If events keep
    57→	// showing up with no break for this time, we'll trigger a push.
    58→	debounceMax time.Duration
    59→
    60→	// enableEDSDebounce indicates whether EDS pushes should be debounced.
    61→	enableEDSDebounce bool
    62→}

    64→// DiscoveryServer is Pilot's gRPC implementation for Envoy's xds APIs
    65→type DiscoveryServer struct {
    66→	// Env is the model environment.
    67→	Env *model.Environment
    68→
    69→	// Generators allow customizing the generated config, based on the client metadata.
    70→	// Key is the generator type - will match the Generator metadata to set the per-connection
    71→	// default generator, or the combination of Generator metadata and TypeUrl to select a
    72→	// different generator for a type.
    73→	// Normal istio clients use the default generator - will not be impacted by this.
    74→	Generators map[string]model.XdsResourceGenerator
    75→
    76→	// ProxyNeedsPush is a function that determines whether a push can be completely skipped. Individual generators
    77→	// may also choose to not send any updates.
    78→	ProxyNeedsPush func(proxy *model.Proxy, req *model.PushRequest) (*model.PushRequest, bool)
    79→
    80→	// concurrentPushLimit is a semaphore that limits the amount of concurrent XDS pushes.
    81→	concurrentPushLimit chan struct{}
    82→	// RequestRateLimit limits the number of new XDS requests allowed. This helps prevent thundering hurd of incoming requests.
    83→	RequestRateLimit *rate.Limiter
    84→
    85→	// InboundUpdates describes the number of configuration updates the discovery server has received
    86→	InboundUpdates *atomic.Int64
    87→	// CommittedUpdates describes the number of configuration updates the discovery server has
    88→	// received, process, and stored in the push context. If this number is less than InboundUpdates,
    89→	// there are updates we have not yet processed.
    90→	// Note: This does not mean that all proxies have received these configurations; it is strictly
    91→	// the push context, which means that the next push to a proxy will receive this configuration.
    92→	CommittedUpdates *atomic.Int64
    93→
    94→	// pushChannel is the buffer used for debouncing.
    95→	// after debouncing the pushRequest will be sent to pushQueue
    96→	pushChannel chan *model.PushRequest
    97→
    98→	// pushQueue is the buffer that used after debounce and before the real xds push.
    99→	pushQueue *PushQueue

   101→	// debugHandlers is the list of all the supported debug handlers.

   140→// NewDiscoveryServer creates DiscoveryServer that sources data from Pilot's internal mesh data structures
   141→func NewDiscoveryServer(env *model.Environment, clusterAliases map[string]string, debugger *krt.DebugHandler) *DiscoveryServer {
   142→	out := &DiscoveryServer{
   143→		Env:                 env,
   144→		Generators:          map[string]model.XdsResourceGenerator{},
   145→		ProxyNeedsPush:      DefaultProxyNeedsPush,
   146→		concurrentPushLimit: make(chan struct{}, features.PushThrottle),
   147→		RequestRateLimit:    rate.NewLimiter(rate.Limit(features.RequestLimit), 1),
   148→		InboundUpdates:      atomic.NewInt64(0),
   149→		CommittedUpdates:    atomic.NewInt64(0),
   150→		pushChannel:         make(chan *model.PushRequest, 10),
   151→		pushQueue:           NewPushQueue(),
   152→		debugHandlers:       map[string]string{},
   153→		adsClients:          map[string]*Connection{},
   154→		krtDebugger:         debugger,
   155→		DebounceOptions: DebounceOptions{
   156→			DebounceAfter:     features.DebounceAfter,
   157→			debounceMax:       features.DebounceMax,
   158→			enableEDSDebounce: features.EnableEDSDebounce,
   159→		},
   160→		Cache:              env.Cache,
   161→		DiscoveryStartTime: processStartTime,
   162→	}
   163→
   164→	out.ClusterAliases = make(map[cluster.ID]cluster.ID)
   165→	for alias := range clusterAliases {
   166→		out.ClusterAliases[cluster.ID(alias)] = cluster.ID(clusterAliases[alias])
   167→	}
   168→
   169→	out.initJwksResolver()
   170→
   171→	return out
   172→}

   214→func (s *DiscoveryServer) Start(stopCh <-chan struct{}) {
   215→	go s.WorkloadEntryController.Run(stopCh)
   216→	go s.handleUpdates(stopCh)
   217→	go s.periodicRefreshMetrics(stopCh)
   218→	go s.sendPushes(stopCh)
   219→	go s.Cache.Run(stopCh)
   220→}

   222→// Push metrics are updated periodically (10s default)
   223→func (s *DiscoveryServer) periodicRefreshMetrics(stopCh <-chan struct{}) {
   224→	ticker := time.NewTicker(periodicRefreshMetrics)
   225→	defer ticker.Stop()
   226→	for {
   227→		select {
   228→		case <-ticker.C:
   229→			push := s.globalPushContext()
   230→			model.LastPushMutex.Lock()
   231→			if model.LastPushStatus != push {
   232→				model.LastPushStatus = push
   233→				push.UpdateMetrics()
   234→				out, _ := model.LastPushStatus.StatusJSON()
   235→				if string(out) != "{}" {
   236→					log.Infof("Push Status: %s", string(out))
   237→				}
   238→			}
   239→			model.LastPushMutex.Unlock()
   240→		case <-stopCh:
   241→			return
   242→		}
   243→	}
   244→}
   245→
   246→// dropCacheForRequest clears the cache in response to a push request
   247→func (s *DiscoveryServer) dropCacheForRequest(req *model.PushRequest) {
   248→	// If we don't know what updated, cannot safely cache. Clear the whole cache
   249→	if req.Forced {
   250→		s.Cache.ClearAll()
   251→	} else {
   252→		// Otherwise, just clear the updated configs
   253→		s.Cache.Clear(req.ConfigsUpdated)
   254→	}
   255→}
   256→
   257→// Push is called to push changes on config updates using ADS.
   258→func (s *DiscoveryServer) Push(req *model.PushRequest) {
   259→	if !req.Full {
   260→		req.Push = s.globalPushContext()
   261→		s.dropCacheForRequest(req)
   262→		s.AdsPushAll(req)
   263→		return
   264→	}
   265→	// Reset the status during the push.
   266→	oldPushContext := s.globalPushContext()
   267→	if oldPushContext != nil {
   268→		oldPushContext.OnConfigChange()
   269→		// Push the previous push Envoy metrics.
   270→		envoyfilter.RecordMetrics()
   271→	}
   272→	// PushContext is reset after a config change. Previous status is
   273→	// saved.
   274→	t0 := time.Now()
   275→	versionLocal := s.NextVersion()
   276→	push := s.initPushContext(req, oldPushContext, versionLocal)
   277→	initContextTime := time.Since(t0)
   278→	log.Debugf("InitContext %v for push took %s", versionLocal, initContextTime)
   279→	pushContextInitTime.Record(initContextTime.Seconds())
   280→
   281→	req.Push = push
   282→	s.AdsPushAll(req)
   283→}

   296→var fullPushLog = istiolog.RegisterScope("fullpush", "logs details about why Istio is triggering a full push")
   297→
   298→// ConfigUpdate implements ConfigUpdater interface, used to request pushes.
   299→func (s *DiscoveryServer) ConfigUpdate(req *model.PushRequest) {
   300→	if features.EnableUnsafeAssertions {
   301→		if model.HasConfigsOfKind(req.ConfigsUpdated, kind.Service) {
   302→			panic("assertion failed kind.Service can not be set in ConfigKey")
   303→		}
   304→	}
   305→	if model.HasConfigsOfKind(req.ConfigsUpdated, kind.Address) {
   306→		// This is a bit like clearing EDS cache on EndpointShard update. Because Address
   307→		// types are fetched dynamically, they are not part of the same protections, so we need to clear
   308→		// the cache.
   309→		s.Cache.ClearAll()
   310→	}
   311→	inboundConfigUpdates.Increment()
   312→	s.InboundUpdates.Inc()
   313→	if req.Full && fullPushLog.DebugEnabled() {
   314→		configs := slices.Sort(slices.Map(req.ConfigsUpdated.UnsortedList(), model.ConfigKey.String))
   315→		reasons := maps.Keys(req.Reason)
   316→		fullPushLog.Debugf("full push triggered configs=%v reasons=%v", configs, reasons)
   317→	}
   318→	s.pushChannel <- req
   319→}
   320→
   321→// Debouncing and push request happens in a separate thread, it uses locks
   322→// and we want to avoid complications, ConfigUpdate may already hold other locks.
   323→// handleUpdates processes events from pushChannel
   324→// It ensures that at minimum minQuiet time has elapsed since the last event before processing it.
   325→// It also ensures that at most maxDelay is elapsed between receiving an event and processing it.
   326→func (s *DiscoveryServer) handleUpdates(stopCh <-chan struct{}) {
   327→	debounce(s.pushChannel, stopCh, s.DebounceOptions, s.Push, s.CommittedUpdates)
   328→}
   329→
   330→// The debounce helper function is implemented to enable mocking
   331→func debounce(ch chan *model.PushRequest, stopCh <-chan struct{}, opts DebounceOptions, pushFn func(req *model.PushRequest), updateSent *atomic.Int64) {
   332→	var timeChan <-chan time.Time
   333→	var startDebounce time.Time
   334→	var lastConfigUpdateTime time.Time
   335→
   336→	pushCounter := 0
   337→	debouncedEvents := 0
   338→
   339→	// Keeps track of the push requests. If updates are debounce they will be merged.
   340→	var req *model.PushRequest
   341→
   342→	free := true
   343→	freeCh := make(chan struct{}, 1)
   344→
   345→	push := func(req *model.PushRequest, debouncedEvents int, startDebounce time.Time) {
   346→		pushFn(req)
   347→		updateSent.Add(int64(debouncedEvents))
   348→		debounceTime.Record(time.Since(startDebounce).Seconds())
   349→		freeCh <- struct{}{}
   350→	}
   351→
   352→	pushWorker := func() {
   353→		eventDelay := time.Since(startDebounce)
   354→		quietTime := time.Since(lastConfigUpdateTime)
   355→		// it has been too long or quiet enough
   356→		if eventDelay >= opts.debounceMax || quietTime >= opts.DebounceAfter {
   357→			if req != nil {
   358→				pushCounter++
   359→				if req.ConfigsUpdated == nil {
   360→					log.Infof("Push debounce stable[%d] %d for reason %s: %v since last change, %v since last push, full=%v",
   361→						pushCounter, debouncedEvents, reasonsUpdated(req),
   362→						quietTime, eventDelay, req.Full)
   363→				} else {
   364→					log.Infof("Push debounce stable[%d] %d for config %s: %v since last change, %v since last push, full=%v",
   365→						pushCounter, debouncedEvents, configsUpdated(req),
   366→						quietTime, eventDelay, req.Full)
   367→				}
   368→				free = false
   369→				go push(req, debouncedEvents, startDebounce)
   370→				req = nil
   371→				debouncedEvents = 0
   372→			}
   373→		} else {
   374→			timeChan = time.After(opts.DebounceAfter - quietTime)
   375→		}
   376→	}
   377→
   378→	for {
   379→		select {
   380→		case <-freeCh:
   381→			free = true
   382→			pushWorker()
   383→		case r := <-ch:
   384→			// If reason is not set, record it as an unknown reason
   385→			if len(r.Reason) == 0 {
   386→				r.Reason = model.NewReasonStats(model.UnknownTrigger)
   387→			}
   388→			if !opts.enableEDSDebounce && !r.Full {
   389→				// trigger push now, just for EDS
   390→				go func(req *model.PushRequest) {
   391→					pushFn(req)
   392→					updateSent.Inc()
   393→				}(r)
   394→				continue
   395→			}
   396→
   397→			lastConfigUpdateTime = time.Now()
   398→			if debouncedEvents == 0 {
   399→				timeChan = time.After(opts.DebounceAfter)
   400→				startDebounce = lastConfigUpdateTime
   401→			}
   402→			debouncedEvents++
   403→
   404→			req = req.Merge(r)
   405→		case <-timeChan:
   406→			if free {
   407→				pushWorker()
   408→			}
   409→		case <-stopCh:
   410→			return
   411→		}
   412→	}
   413→}
   414→
   415→func configsUpdated(req *model.PushRequest) string {
   416→	configs := ""
   417→	for key := range req.ConfigsUpdated {
   418→		configs += key.String()
   419→		break
   420→	}
   421→	if len(req.ConfigsUpdated) > 1 {
   422→		more := " and " + strconv.Itoa(len(req.ConfigsUpdated)-1) + " more configs"
   423→		configs += more
   424→	}
   425→	return configs
   426→}
   427→
   428→func reasonsUpdated(req *model.PushRequest) string {
   429→	var (
   430→		reason0, reason1            model.TriggerReason
   431→		reason0Cnt, reason1Cnt, idx int
   432→	)
   433→	for r, cnt := range req.Reason {
   434→		if idx == 0 {
   435→			reason0, reason0Cnt = r, cnt
   436→		} else if idx == 1 {
   437→			reason1, reason1Cnt = r, cnt
   438→		} else {
   439→			break
   440→		}
   441→		idx++
   442→	}
   443→
   444→	switch len(req.Reason) {
   445→	case 0:
   446→		return "unknown"
   447→	case 1:
   448→		return fmt.Sprintf("%s:%d", reason0, reason0Cnt)
   449→	case 2:
   450→		return fmt.Sprintf("%s:%d and %s:%d", reason0, reason0Cnt, reason1, reason1Cnt)
   451→	default:
   452→		return fmt.Sprintf("%s:%d and %d(%d) more reasons", reason0, reason0Cnt, len(req.Reason)-1,
   453→			req.Reason.Count()-reason0Cnt)
   454→	}
   455→}

   457→func doSendPushes(stopCh <-chan struct{}, semaphore chan struct{}, queue *PushQueue) {
   458→	for {
   459→		select {
   460→		case <-stopCh:
   461→			return
   462→		default:
   463→			// We can send to it until it is full, then it will block until a pushes finishes and reads from it.
   464→			// This limits the number of pushes that can happen concurrently
   465→			semaphore <- struct{}{}
   466→
   467→			// Get the next proxy to push. This will block if there are no updates required.
   468→			client, push, shuttingdown := queue.Dequeue()
   469→			if shuttingdown {
   470→				return
   471→			}
   472→			recordPushTriggers(push.Reason)
   473→			// Signals that a push is done by reading from the semaphore, allowing another send on it.
   474→			doneFunc := func() {
   475→				queue.MarkDone(client)
   476→				<-semaphore
   477→			}
   478→
   479→			proxiesQueueTime.Record(time.Since(push.Start).Seconds())
   480→			var closed <-chan struct{}
   481→			if client.deltaStream != nil {
   482→				closed = client.deltaStream.Context().Done()
   483→			} else {
   484→				closed = client.StreamDone()
   485→			}
   486→			go func() {
   487→				pushEv := &Event{
   488→					pushRequest: push,
   489→					done:        doneFunc,
   490→				}
   491→
   492→				select {
   493→				case client.PushCh() <- pushEv:
   494→					return
   495→				case <-closed: // grpc stream was closed
   496→					doneFunc()
   497→					log.Infof("Client closed connection %v", client.ID())
   498→				}
   499→			}()
   500→		}
   501→	}
   502→}

   504→// initPushContext creates a global push context and stores it on the environment. Note: while this
   505→// method is technically thread safe (there are no data races), it should not be called in parallel;
   506→// if it is, then we may start two push context creations (say A, and B), but then write them in
   507→// reverse order, leaving us with a final version of A, which may be incomplete.
   508→func (s *DiscoveryServer) initPushContext(req *model.PushRequest, oldPushContext *model.PushContext, version string) *model.PushContext {
   509→	push := model.NewPushContext()
   510→	push.PushVersion = version
   511→	push.JwtKeyResolver = s.JwtKeyResolver
   512→	push.InitContext(s.Env, oldPushContext, req)
   513→
   514→	s.dropCacheForRequest(req)
   515→	s.Env.SetPushContext(push)
   516→
   517→	return push
   518→}

   520→func (s *DiscoveryServer) sendPushes(stopCh <-chan struct{}) {
   521→	doSendPushes(stopCh, s.concurrentPushLimit, s.pushQueue)
   522→}

   530→// Clients returns all currently connected clients. This method can be safely called concurrently,
   531→// but care should be taken with the underlying objects (ie model.Proxy) to ensure proper locking.
   532→// This method returns only fully initialized connections; for all connections, use AllClients
   533→func (s *DiscoveryServer) Clients() []*Connection {
   534→	s.adsClientsMutex.RLock()
   535→	defer s.adsClientsMutex.RUnlock()
   536→	clients := make([]*Connection, 0, len(s.adsClients))
   537→	for _, con := range s.adsClients {
   538→		select {
   539→		case <-con.InitializedCh():
   540→		default:
   541→			// Initialization not complete, skip
   542→			continue
   543→		}
   544→		clients = append(clients, con)
   545→	}
   546→	return clients
   547→}
   548→
   549→// SortedClients returns all currently connected clients in an ordered manner.
   550→// Sorting order priority is as follows: ClusterID, Namespace, ID.
   551→func (s *DiscoveryServer) SortedClients() []*Connection {
   552→	clients := s.Clients()
   553→	sort.Slice(clients, func(i, j int) bool {
   554→		if clients[i].proxy.GetClusterID().String() < clients[j].proxy.GetClusterID().String() {
   555→			return true
   556→		}
   557→		if clients[i].proxy.GetNamespace() < clients[j].proxy.GetNamespace() {
   558→			return true
   559→		}
   560→		return clients[i].proxy.GetID() < clients[j].proxy.GetID()
   561→	})
   562→	return clients
   563→}
   564→
   565→// AllClients returns all connected clients, per Clients, but additionally includes uninitialized connections
   566→// Warning: callers must take care not to rely on the con.proxy field being set
   567→func (s *DiscoveryServer) AllClients() []*Connection {
   568→	s.adsClientsMutex.RLock()
   569→	defer s.adsClientsMutex.RUnlock()
   570→	return maps.Values(s.adsClients)
   571→}
   572→
   573→func (s *DiscoveryServer) WaitForRequestLimit(ctx context.Context) error {
   574→	if s.RequestRateLimit.Limit() == 0 {
   575→		// Allow opt out when rate limiting is set to 0qps
   576→		return nil
   577→	}
   578→	// Give a bit of time for queue to clear out, but if not fail fast. Client will connect to another
   579→	// instance in best case, or retry with backoff.
   580→	wait, cancel := context.WithTimeout(ctx, time.Second)
   581→	defer cancel()
   582→	return s.RequestRateLimit.Wait(wait)
   583→}

   585→func (s *DiscoveryServer) NextVersion() string {
   586→	return time.Now().Format(time.RFC3339) + "/" + strconv.FormatUint(s.pushVersion.Inc(), 10)
   587→}
